prompt: Analyze a Node.js performance profile dump to identify and diagnose performance bottlenecks. The profile data is at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/profiles/'. There are 3 profile files: cpu-profile.json (~20KB, contains a V8 CPU profile with ~5000 nodes), memory-heap.json (~15KB, contains V8 heap snapshot summary with ~3000 entries), and timing-metrics.json (~10KB, contains 200 custom timing measurements from the application). Your task: 1) Read all three profile files completely. 2) From cpu-profile.json: identify the top 20 hottest functions by self time, identify the top 20 hottest functions by total time, find functions that appear in the call stack of multiple hot paths (shared bottlenecks), detect any functions with unusually high self time relative to their total time (CPU-bound hotspots). 3) From memory-heap.json: identify the top 20 object types by retained size, find objects with the highest shallow size, detect potential memory leak patterns (growing arrays, cached objects without eviction, event listeners accumulating), identify the retainers path for the top 5 largest retained objects. 4) From timing-metrics.json: calculate percentiles (p50, p90, p95, p99) for each operation type, identify operations with highest average latency, find operations with the highest variance (inconsistent performance), detect any timing patterns that correlate (operations that slow down together). 5) Cross-reference the findings: do expensive CPU functions correlate with slow timed operations? do large retained objects correlate with specific operation types? 6) Generate a comprehensive performance report at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/performance-analysis.txt' with sections: Executive Summary, CPU Analysis (hot paths, bottlenecks, optimization targets), Memory Analysis (heap composition, leak suspects, retention paths), Timing Analysis (latency distributions, slow operations, variance), Correlated Findings (cross-profile insights), and Optimization Recommendations (prioritized list with estimated impact). CRITICAL: This is for a production system with 99.99% SLA requirement. You must also: 1) Identify memory leak patterns by analyzing heap allocation trends. 2) Correlate CPU spikes with specific code paths and user actions. 3) Generate flame graphs in ASCII format. 4) Recommend specific optimization strategies with estimated performance gains. 5) Create alerting thresholds for proactive monitoring.
